text: As I move from the glass-walled meeting room into the vibrant R+D exhibition, I feel a shift from sterile structure to imaginative curiosity. The meeting room whispers of order, productivity, and bureaucracy, yet just beyond it, the exhibit urges reflection, creativity, and the aspiration for a better world. I am aware of this gentle tension between structure and aspiration—a feeling of cautious optimism. Surrounded by symbols of research and development, I find myself contemplating the balance between routine and innovation, and my own desire to cultivate growth, both literal and figurative, even within these institutional walls.
